input.background: A nuclear plant is located in an area susceptible to both high winds and earthquakes. From the historical data, the mean frequency of large earthquakes capable of damaging important plant structures is one every 50 years. The corresponding frequency of damaging high winds is once in 25 years. During a strong earthquake, the probability of structure damage is 0.1. During high winds, the damage probability is 0.05. Assume that earthquakes and high winds can be described by independent Poisson random variables and that the damages caused by these events are independent.
input.question: What is the probability of building damage during the 10-year period?

output.answer: We assume that there will not be multiple occurrences of earthquakes and high winds during the 10-year period. Therefore, the probability of building damage can be written as $$\begin{aligned} P(C) & =P(\ \text { damage/earthquakes }) P(\ \text { earthquakes }) \\ & +\mathrm{P}(\ \text { damage/wind }) \mathrm{P}(\ \text { wind }) \\ & -\mathrm{P}(\ \text { damage/earthquakes and wind }) \mathrm{P}(\ \text { earthquake and wind }) \\ & =P(\ \text { damage/earthquakes }) P(\ \text { earthquakes }) \\ & +\mathrm{P}(\ \text { damage/wind }) \mathrm{P}(\ \text { wind }) \\ & -\mathrm{P}(\ \text { damage/earthquakes }) \mathrm{P}(\ \text { damage/wind }) \mathrm{P}(\ \text { earthquake and wind }) \\ & =\left(1-\mathrm{e}^{-0.2}\right)(0.1)+\left(1-\mathrm{e}^{-0.4}\right)(0.05)-(0.05)(0.1)(0.06) \\ & =0.0343 \end{aligned}$$.

meta_info.chapter: Chapter 2: Distribution Functions and Its Applications
